title: "Uuntu 8.10 network manager problems (file /etc/resolv.conf)"
date: 2009-03-29
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless

### Post by casdio on 2009-03-29
Hi.

My /etc/resolv.conf file changes himself. I asked a friend to let me see his file and it said:
> #Generated by network manager

Mine says:
> # Dynamic resolv.conf( 5) file for glibc resolver( 3) generated by resolvconf(8 )
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN

Anyone knows what the problem can be? I don't want it to change alone :|

---

### Post by tonym on 2009-03-29
Not sure what your problem is.  Ubuntu generates resolv.conf automatically.

If you get your IP from DHCP this should also give you the DNS servers.

If you have a static IP you should include a stanza in /etc/network/interfaces eg

dns-nameservers w.x.y.z

Regards

Tony Middleton

---

### Post by casdio on 2009-03-29
Here is my 'default' /etc/resolv.conf file
```
# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 193.137.17.6
nameserver 193.137.17.46
nameserver 192.168.1.1
search netcabo.pt
```

And my /etc/network/interfaces file
```
auto lo
iface lo inet loopback
```
